patch.value: Runtime-extension snapshot caches go stale in long-lived in-process embeddings (install invisible to next invocation)

cli/main.ts holds module-level single-entry caches (runtimeExtensionSnapshotCache, runtimeExtensionDiscoverySnapshotCache, activeRuntimeExtensionCommandDescriptors) keyed only by pmRoot with no mtime/invalidation seam. They exist to dedupe discovery within ONE invocation, but any host that keeps the module alive across invocations (in-process test runners, future SDK embedding of runPmCli) observes stale extension state: 'pm install templates' followed by 'pm templates --help' in the same module instance exits 2 with 'not installed'. One-shot pm processes never hit this; tests/helpers/cliRunner.ts runInProcessDistCli cache-busts the whole bundle per call purely to dodge this class. Fix: reset the three caches at runPmCli entry — intra-invocation dedupe preserved, spawn semantics restored for every embedding. MCP server is unaffected (separate runAction/withActiveExtensions path).

runPmCli resets all module-level runtime-extension caches on entry; an in-process install->use sequence sees the installed extension; no added per-invocation cost for one-shot processes beyond the reset itself

value.path: src/cli/main.ts:reset-on-entry seam for runtime-extension snapshot caches

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Second class member found while validating the fix: core/extensions/index.ts active-extension registries (hooks/commands/parsers/preflight/services/renderers/registrations + command context/result) also persist across invocations — the no-extensions activation path resets only services+descriptors, so an extension activated by a previous in-process invocation leaks into workspaces where it is not installed (observed: kit extension's list --kit-note registration rejecting core 'pm list --type' with usage error). Fix extended: new resetActiveExtensionRuntimeState() in core/extensions/index.ts, called from the same runPmCli entry reset.

author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: an in-process install->use sequence sees the installed extension; long-lived embeddings get spawn-equivalent extension state per invocation

runPmCli now resets the snapshot caches and all active-extension registries on entry (resetActiveExtensionRuntimeState in core/extensions/index.ts); install->use verified in-worker (templates/guide-shell repros green); one-shot latency unchanged (~112-166ms --version)

message: Fix shipped with the pm-kvd0 bridge lane

author: maintainer-agent
